findings.message: ParentZoneShell has no self-contained role guard. It relies entirely on InboundMain to hide it via visibility. For defense-in-depth child safety, the shell should verify _profile is a parent in its own _ready() or setup() and hide/disable itself if not. While Godot desktop apps make it difficult for kids to bypass visibility, this is a safety best practice that protects against future navigation paths or composition changes.

main.gd uses string-based .call('setup', ...) instead of typed method calls (lines 66-69). The @onready vars are typed as Control instead of their specific shell classes (CreateShell, PlayShell, etc). This means a setup() signature mismatch would only surface at runtime. Consider casting to the concrete shell type or using a shared shell base class with a typed setup signature.

Undo and safe-restore buttons are present in all 4 shells with Polish labels but have no pressed.connect() handlers. They are non-functional stubs. Acceptable for TASK-008 scope (shell scaffolding), but should be tracked for TASK-021 (build canvas tools).

Each shell has its own _t() helper with identical logic and duplicated Polish fallback dictionaries. Extract to a shared base class or utility function. The common keys 'ui.common.undo' and 'ui.common.safe_restore' are duplicated across all 4 shells.
ShellNavigator uses old-style emit_signal('shell_changed', shell_id) instead of Godot 4.x idiomatic shell_changed.emit(shell_id).

acceptance_criteria.adapters_call_inbound_ports: PASS - All shells receive ports via setup() DI. No shell creates domain aggregates or entities. Dependencies are inbound port interfaces (CreateProjectFromTemplatePort, RunPlaytestPort, PublishToFamilyLibraryPort, SetParentalControlsPort) plus LocalizationPolicyPort.
acceptance_criteria.shells_navigable: PASS - ShellNavigator manages shell visibility. Main nav buttons route among Create, Play, Library, and Parent Zone. Polish labels on all nav buttons.
acceptance_criteria.kid_mode_hides_parent: PARTIAL - InboundMain._is_parent() gates both parent shell visibility and nav button visibility. But ParentZoneShell has no self-guard (see medium finding).

checklist.hexagonal_boundaries: PASS - Shells only reference inbound ports and LocalizationPolicyPort. ShellNavigator extends RefCounted. PlayerProfile is accepted by shells but is effectively a read-only value object (profile_id, role, age_band) — acceptable for adapter layer role decisions.
checklist.kid_safety_and_parental_controls: PARTIAL - Parent zone is visibility-gated in main.gd. Defense-in-depth self-guard missing from ParentZoneShell (medium finding).
checklist.polish_localization: PASS - All fallback strings are Polish (pl-PL). _t() helper correctly delegates to LocalizationPolicyPort first. Navigation labels: Twórz, Graj, Biblioteka, Strefa rodzica.
checklist.touch_targets: PASS - All buttons across all shells meet 44px height minimum via custom_minimum_size.
checklist.cross_agent_review: This review constitutes the required cross-agent review for TASK-008.

positive_notes: Consistent DI pattern across all shells: setup() with port interfaces, returning self for fluent chaining.
Polish-first UI with proper fallback pattern — localization port preferred, hardcoded pl-PL as fallback.
Clean scene structure: consistent VBoxContainer > Header + Actions layout with 16px spacing across all shells.
ShellNavigator is a clean RefCounted utility that decouples shell transitions from shell implementations.
Touch-friendly 44px minimum heights on all interactive buttons.
summary: TASK-008 delivers clean inbound adapter shells that properly use DI and preserve hexagonal boundaries. Polish-first localization and touch-target compliance are met. Two medium findings: ParentZoneShell needs a self-contained role guard for defense-in-depth child safety, and main.gd uses string-based .call() instead of typed shell references. Three low-severity findings for code style and DRY. Overall: APPROVE. The parent guard finding should be addressed in a follow-up before TASK-029 (parent controls).
